<!-- auto-generated by cmd/docgen — run `make docs` to regenerate. DO NOT EDIT. -->

# guild MCP tool catalog

Every tool a registered MCP client sees. 43 tools.

## Tools

- [`guild_session_start`](#guild_session_start) — Call FIRST — before any other guild tool will work.
- [`guild_set_project`](#guild_set_project) — Switch the active project for the rest of this session.
- [`guild_status`](#guild_status) — On-demand dashboard mirroring guild_session_start — last brief, oath, echoes, top bounty, parallelism.
- [`lore_appraise`](#lore_appraise) — Search lore before storing new knowledge or spawning research subagents.
- [`lore_catalog`](#lore_catalog) — Bulk-import .md files under DIR as lore entries.
- [`lore_commune`](#lore_commune) — Health report for oath bloat and duplicate lore.
- [`lore_coverage_reconcile`](#lore_coverage_reconcile) — Reset meta.vector_coverage_den to the live COUNT(*) WHERE status NOT IN ('archived','parked') and report before/after values.
- [`lore_dossier`](#lore_dossier) — Compile ~2000-token project context for subagent spawn prompts.
- [`lore_echoes`](#lore_echoes) — List stale/decayed entries the next agent should review or reforge.
- [`lore_embed_rebuild`](#lore_embed_rebuild) — Delete all lore_vectors rows for the active project, flip every active entry's vector_state to 'pending', then encode each entry and insert the resulting int8 vectors.
- [`lore_health`](#lore_health) — Print the embedder health section: model_id, tokenizer_hash, runtime_version, dim, coverage (num/den and percent), pending count, stale count, last encode error (if any), last successful encode timestamp, and rolling embed_error_count.
- [`lore_inquest`](#lore_inquest) — Audit the oath wall for narrative-bloat principles (>60 words).
- [`lore_inscribe`](#lore_inscribe) — Store knowledge that transcends the current task — patterns, decisions, research that outlive the quest.
- [`lore_link`](#lore_link) — Create an informs provenance edge between two entries.
- [`lore_list`](#lore_list) — Browse entries with optional filters.
- [`lore_meld`](#lore_meld) — Find duplicate lore entries across projects.
- [`lore_oath`](#lore_oath) — List all behavioral principles for the active project.
- [`lore_reforge`](#lore_reforge) — Mark an older entry superseded by a newer one.
- [`lore_ripples`](#lore_ripples) — Walk the provenance graph from a seed entry via entry_links.
- [`lore_seal`](#lore_seal) — Seal (archive) an entry from active circulation.
- [`lore_study`](#lore_study) — Get full detail of one entry: summary, metadata, linked entries.
- [`lore_unlink`](#lore_unlink) — Remove an informs (or supersedes/contradicts) edge from the provenance graph.
- [`lore_update`](#lore_update) — Edit an entry's title, status, tags, kind, topic, or summary.
- [`lore_whispers`](#lore_whispers) — List lore entries in the idea pipeline — entries with kind=idea and an early status.
- [`quest_accept`](#quest_accept) — Atomically claim a quest so two agents do not take the same work, then return the current spec and recent state.
- [`quest_active`](#quest_active) — List every in-progress quest across all projects.
- [`quest_bounties`](#quest_bounties) — On-demand session snapshot: brief, oath, echoes, top task, and parallel candidates.
- [`quest_brief`](#quest_brief) — Session-end briefing for the next agent.
- [`quest_campfire`](#quest_campfire) — Save a structured working-state checkpoint (hypothesis, tried, next, token warning) before the context compacts.
- [`quest_clear`](#quest_clear) — Fulfill a quest.
- [`quest_epic`](#quest_epic) — Set the campaign name on a batch of quests.
- [`quest_forfeit`](#quest_forfeit) — Release a claimed quest back to the queue.
- [`quest_fulfill`](#quest_fulfill) — Fulfill a quest.
- [`quest_guild`](#quest_guild) — Per-project quest summary grouped by campaign: counts of next, in-progress, blocked, and done quests.
- [`quest_journal`](#quest_journal) — Task-scoped scratchpad.
- [`quest_list`](#quest_list) — All open tasks.
- [`quest_orders`](#quest_orders) — List quests currently assigned to a given agent owner.
- [`quest_post`](#quest_post) — Create a quest another agent can accept without human follow-up — well-specced quest = no human follow-up needed to execute it.
- [`quest_pulse`](#quest_pulse) — Rework rate, churn rate, hot files, untested quests.
- [`quest_scroll`](#quest_scroll) — Full quest history: status, journal, timeline.
- [`quest_search`](#quest_search) — BM25+stopwords full-text search over quest subjects and spec notes.
- [`quest_summon`](#quest_summon) — Reassign a quest to a named teammate agent.
- [`quest_update`](#quest_update) — Modify a quest's spec after post.

## `guild_session_start`

Call FIRST — before any other guild tool will work. Sets the active project, loads the briefing, oath, and top task, and defaults later guild tools to it (you do not need to pass project to them again).

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "brief_only": {
      "description": "when true, return only the last briefing",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "description": "directory basename, e.g. 'guild'. Optional — when omitted, auto-infers from the MCP server's cwd via git-toplevel lookup. Pass explicitly to override or when cwd auto-inference can't reach the project. Auto-infer handles git worktrees — falls back to the main-repo path if the worktree path isn't registered.",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `guild_set_project`

Switch the active project for the rest of this session. Use to work across projects in one MCP connection.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "description": "directory basename to activate (e.g. 'guild') — REQUIRED",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "project"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `guild_status`

On-demand dashboard mirroring guild_session_start — last brief, oath, echoes, top bounty, parallelism. Call anytime to re-orient mid-session without re-bootstrapping.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "brief_only": {
      "description": "when true, return only the last briefing",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "description": "project override; defaults to active session project",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_appraise`

Search lore before storing new knowledge or spawning research subagents. Returns ranked entries with project, kind, age, and summary — if current results exist, use them instead of re-deriving.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "all_projects": {
      "description": "search every project (recommended for research)",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "limit": {
      "description": "max results (default 10)",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "project": {
      "description": "project override; ignored when all_projects=true",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "query": {
      "description": "search query; BM25+recency+title-boost ranked",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "since": {
      "description": "entries within window: 7d|2w|1m",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "query"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_catalog`

Bulk-import .md files under DIR as lore entries. Idempotent on re-runs.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "dir": {
      "description": "directory to walk for .md files",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "kind": {
      "description": "override kind (default inferred from path)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "tags": {
      "description": "comma-separated tags applied to all imported entries",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "topic": {
      "description": "override per-file topic; default: file stem",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "dir"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_commune`

Health report for oath bloat and duplicate lore. fix=true auto-remediates severe issues.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "all_projects": {
      "description": "check across every project",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "fix": {
      "description": "apply safe auto-remediation",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_coverage_reconcile`

Reset meta.vector_coverage_den to the live COUNT(*) WHERE status NOT IN ('archived','parked') and report before/after values. Corrects num > den drift that produces coverage > 100%. Backfill also runs this automatically, so this command is a manual escape hatch.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_dossier`

Compile ~2000-token project context for subagent spawn prompts. Use when delegating multi-file work.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_echoes`

List stale/decayed entries the next agent should review or reforge. Cross-checks file_path modification times when --git-aware is set.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "git_aware": {
      "description": "also flag entries whose file_path was modified after creation",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_embed_rebuild`

Delete all lore_vectors rows for the active project, flip every active entry's vector_state to 'pending', then encode each entry and insert the resulting int8 vectors. Safe under concurrent MCP servers: uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE for the reset phase and INSERT OR IGNORE for each vector write (ADR-003 invariants).

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_health`

Print the embedder health section: model_id, tokenizer_hash, runtime_version, dim, coverage (num/den and percent), pending count, stale count, last encode error (if any), last successful encode timestamp, and rolling embed_error_count.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_inquest`

Audit the oath wall for narrative-bloat principles (>60 words). Demote bloated principles to decisions.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "all_projects": {
      "description": "scan every project, not just current",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_inscribe`

Store knowledge that transcends the current task — patterns, decisions, research that outlive the quest. Call lore_appraise first; pass informs=[IDs] for entries that informed this one to create provenance edges at write-time. Cross-project dedup and principle-hygiene warnings are built in.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "informs": {
      "description": "source entry IDs (LORE-N, ENTRY-N, or bare N) that inform this entry — creates informs provenance edges at write-time",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "kind": {
      "description": "one of: idea|research|decision|observation|principle",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "needs_review": {
      "description": "flag for human review",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "no_warn": {
      "description": "suppress the ≤60-word principle warning",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "prompted_by": {
      "description": "QUEST-N that prompted this inscribe",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "source": {
      "description": "URL or path the knowledge came from",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "strict_project": {
      "description": "scope dedup to the current project only (default: cross-project)",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "summary": {
      "description": "2-3 sentence distillation",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "tags": {
      "description": "semantic tags",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "title": {
      "description": "short distinctive title (search-friendly)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "topic": {
      "description": "topic slug (e.g. 'auth', 'caching')",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "title",
    "kind",
    "summary",
    "topic"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_link`

Create an informs provenance edge between two entries. Cross-project allowed.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "from_id": {
      "description": "source entry id",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "relation": {
      "description": "informs|supersedes|contradicts (default informs)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "to_id": {
      "description": "target entry id — the one being informed by from",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "from_id",
    "to_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_list`

Browse entries with optional filters. Use for inventory; lore_appraise for search.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "kind": {
      "description": "filter by kind",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "status": {
      "description": "filter by status",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "topic": {
      "description": "filter by topic slug",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_meld`

Find duplicate lore entries across projects. Surfaces reforge candidates. Cross-project by default.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "all_projects": {
      "description": "span every project (default when no project set)",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "threshold": {
      "description": "Jaccard near-match threshold (e.g. 0.7); default exact match only",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_oath`

List all behavioral principles for the active project. Auto-loaded by guild_session_start — call to refresh.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_reforge`

Mark an older entry superseded by a newer one. Preserves history — do NOT delete.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "new_id": {
      "description": "the newer entry that replaces it",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "old_id": {
      "description": "the entry being superseded",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "old_id",
    "new_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_ripples`

Walk the provenance graph from a seed entry via entry_links. direction=out follows descendants (what this informed), direction=in follows ancestors (what informed this), direction=both unions both. Default depth=3, max 10.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "depth": {
      "description": "walk depth, default 3, max 10",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "direction": {
      "description": "out|in|both — out follows descendants, in follows ancestors (default out)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "entry_id": {
      "description": "seed entry id (LORE-N, ENTRY-N, or bare N)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "relation": {
      "description": "informs|supersedes|contradicts|all (default all)",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "entry_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_seal`

Seal (archive) an entry from active circulation. History is preserved.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "entry_id": {
      "description": "entry to archive",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "entry_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_study`

Get full detail of one entry: summary, metadata, linked entries. Use after appraise for the deep read.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "entry_id": {
      "description": "numeric entry id (e.g. 542 for ENTRY-542)",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "entry_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_unlink`

Remove an informs (or supersedes/contradicts) edge from the provenance graph. Idempotent: removing a non-existent edge returns success with a 'no matching edge' note.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "from_id": {
      "description": "source entry id",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "relation": {
      "description": "informs|supersedes|contradicts (default informs)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "to_id": {
      "description": "target entry id (the one that was being informed by from)",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "from_id",
    "to_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `lore_update`

Edit an entry's title, status, tags, kind, topic, or summary.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "entry_id": {
      "description": "entry to update",
      "pattern": "^-?[0-9]+$",
      "type": [
        "integer",
        "string"
      ]
    },
    "kind": {
      "description": "reclassify: idea|research|decision|observation|principle",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "status": {
      "description": "new status (current|stale|...)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "summary": {
      "description": "new summary",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "tags": {
      "description": "replace tags (comma-separated)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "title": {
      "description": "new title",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "topic": {
      "description": "new topic slug",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "entry_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

      "type": "string"

{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {

      "description": "when true, return only the last briefing",

## `quest_brief`

Session-end briefing for the next agent. Surfaced by next session's guild_session_start. Call before compact.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "text": {
      "description": "~200-word handoff: what was done, what's next, gotchas",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "text"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_campfire`

Save a structured working-state checkpoint (hypothesis, tried, next, token warning) before the context compacts. Distinct from journal — campfire is a restart beacon.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "hypothesis": {
      "description": "current working hypothesis",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "next": {
      "description": "next step to try",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to snapshot",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "token_warning": {
      "description": "true when context window is running low",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "tried": {
      "description": "approaches tried so far (array)",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_clear`

Fulfill a quest. Report is REQUIRED — commit hash, files, remaining issues. Cascades unblock dependent quests.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to fulfill",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "report": {
      "description": "specific completion report: commit hash, files, issues — REQUIRED",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id",
    "report"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_epic`

Set the campaign name on a batch of quests. Group related work for reporting. Also available as `quest epic` for backward compatibility.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "campaign": {
      "description": "campaign name to apply",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "epic": {
      "description": "campaign name (alias for campaign)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_ids": {
      "description": "quest ids to group under this campaign",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_ids"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_forfeit`

Release a claimed quest back to the queue. Use when blocked or ceding to another agent. Only acts on status=in_progress quests — refuses on done and no-ops on next.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "note": {
      "description": "reason the claim is being released (optional)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to release",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_fulfill`

Fulfill a quest. Report is REQUIRED — commit hash, files, remaining issues. Cascades unblock dependent quests.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to fulfill",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "report": {
      "description": "specific completion report: commit hash, files, issues — REQUIRED",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id",
    "report"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_guild`

Per-project quest summary grouped by campaign: counts of next, in-progress, blocked, and done quests.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_journal`

Task-scoped scratchpad. Dies when the quest clears. If it outlives the task, use lore_inscribe.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN you're working on",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "text": {
      "description": "what you learned while working on THIS quest; dies when the quest clears",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id",
    "text"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_list`

All open tasks. Use after guild_session_start to spot parallelism — unshared files + no deps → spawn agents.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "campaign": {
      "description": "filter by campaign",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "epic": {
      "description": "filter by campaign (alias for campaign)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "status": {
      "description": "filter by status: next|in_progress|blocked|done",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_orders`

List quests currently assigned to a given agent owner. Use to see a teammate's queue or your own.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "agent": {
      "description": "agent id to query (defaults to 'agent')",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_post`

Create a quest another agent can accept without human follow-up — well-specced quest = no human follow-up needed to execute it. Include rich WHY + HOW (rationale, approach, constraints) so a cold agent executes without re-deriving context from chat. Pass spec=... to atomically attach a kind=decision lore entry with full rationale (QUEST-63).

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "acceptance": {
      "description": "acceptance criteria",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "campaign": {
      "description": "campaign name to group related quests",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "depends_on": {
      "description": "QUEST-IDs this depends on",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "effort": {
      "description": "effort label",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "epic": {
      "description": "campaign name (alias for campaign)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "files": {
      "description": "file paths the task will touch",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "priority": {
      "description": "P0|P1|P2|P3 (default P2)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "rework_of": {
      "description": "QUEST-X this is rework of",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "spec": {
      "description": "optional design rationale — atomically inscribes a kind=decision lore entry with full context",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "subject": {
      "description": "one-line task summary",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "subject"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_pulse`

Rework rate, churn rate, hot files, untested quests. Surfaces silent rework no one filed.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "window_days": {
      "description": "window for churn/rework analysis (default 30)",
      "type": "integer"
    }
  },
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_scroll`

Full quest history: status, journal, timeline. Use to pick up a quest someone else was working on.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_search`

BM25+stopwords full-text search over quest subjects and spec notes. When quest vector coverage >= 90%, adds a semantic arm and RRF-fuses (k=60, same gate and fusion as lore_appraise). Returns up to 10 results. Replaces quest list --all | grep.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "limit": {
      "description": "max results (default 10)",
      "type": "integer"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "query": {
      "description": "natural-language search query",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "query"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_summon`

Reassign a quest to a named teammate agent. The quest's owner is updated; a [summon] event is recorded in the timeline.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to delegate",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "to": {
      "description": "target agent id",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id",
    "to"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>

## `quest_update`

Modify a quest's spec after post. Append lists via --files/-a/--depends-on/--blocks; replace via --replace-*; clear via --clear-*.

_no arguments_

<details><summary>Raw JSON schema</summary>

```json
{
  "additionalProperties": false,
  "properties": {
    "acceptance": {
      "description": "append to acceptance criteria",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "blocks": {
      "description": "append to blocks",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "campaign": {
      "description": "new campaign name",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "clear_acceptance": {
      "description": "clear acceptance list",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "clear_blocks": {
      "description": "clear blocks list",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "clear_depends_on": {
      "description": "clear depends_on list",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "clear_files": {
      "description": "clear files list",
      "type": "boolean"
    },
    "depends_on": {
      "description": "append to depends_on",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "effort": {
      "description": "new effort label",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "epic": {
      "description": "new campaign name (alias for campaign)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "files": {
      "description": "append to files",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "priority": {
      "description": "new priority (P0|P1|P2|P3)",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "project": {
      "type": "string"
    },
    "quest_id": {
      "description": "QUEST-NNN to update",
      "type": "string"
    },
    "replace_acceptance": {
      "description": "replace acceptance list entirely",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "replace_blocks": {
      "description": "replace blocks list entirely",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "replace_depends_on": {
      "description": "replace depends_on list entirely",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "replace_files": {
      "description": "replace files list entirely",
      "items": {
        "type": "string"
      },
      "type": [
        "null",
        "array"
      ]
    },
    "subject": {
      "description": "new subject",
      "type": "string"
    }
  },
  "required": [
    "quest_id"
  ],
  "type": "object"
}
```

</details>
